business.headline: Sensex drops 800 points as West Asia tensions hit marketThe BSE Sensex was down 794.65 points, or 1.02%, to 76,768.25 at 10:02 am, while the Nifty 50 dropped 196.55 points, or 0.82%, to 23,800.80.

TCS Q4 today: Will it be a reality check for IT sector after AI shock?Brokerages estimate TCS will report around 1–3% sequential revenue growth, while the broader tier-1 IT pack is likely to post -1% to +1% constant currency growth, pointing to a demand environment that has stabilised but not yet recovered.

Govt cuts airport charges by 25% to support airlines amid West Asia crisisCivil Aviation Minister Ram Mohan Naidu said the Centre will cut landing and parking charges by 25% for domestic flights for a period of three months, in a move aimed at easing operational pressure on airlines.

Sensex, Nifty pare some losses as Iran-US ceasefire holdsThe BSE Sensex declined 354.93 points, or 0.46%, to 77,207.97 in early trade. The Nifty 50 slipped 78.45 points, or 0.33%, to 23,918.90, falling below the psychologically important 24,000 level around 9:20 am.

World Bank raises India growth forecast to 6.6% for 2026-27 from 6.3% earlierEven as growth across the region slows, India continues to stand out as the main engine, providing a sense of stability at a time when the broader economic outlook remains uncertain.

Big rally, bigger question: Buy now or wait? | INTERVIEWMarkets rebounded sharply on Wednesday as lower crude prices lifted sentiment. Sumeet Bagadia of Choice Broking discusses if this is a temporary bounce or a trend shift.

Trump warns of 50% tariffs on countries supplying weapons to IranThe statement comes at a time when global geopolitical risks are already high, especially in West Asia. Any move to impose such tariffs could have wider implications for global trade and diplomatic ties.

Borrowing to survive, facing abuse: How India's credit boom is turning stressfulNearly 4 in 10 borrowers report abusive recovery calls, as a new survey highlights rising repayment stress and growing dependence on credit for everyday needs.

Dalal Street adds Rs 17 lakh crore as Iran-US ceasefire boosts marketsInvestor wealth surged by around Rs 17.1 lakh crore in a single session, taking total BSE market capitalisation to Rs 445.8 lakh crore. The S&P BSE Sensex rallied 2,946.32 points to end at 77,562.90, while the NSE Nifty50 jumped 873.35 points to close at 23,997.35.

Resilient, safe, strong: RBI assures banking system safe amid recent jittersRBI Governor highlighted that the broader banking system remains stable despite global challenges. He said there are no systemic risks to the profitability or health of banks, even as global uncertainty remains due to the West Asia conflict.

Why did RBI keep repo rate unchanged at 5.25%? Know these 5 reasonsNo change in the repo rate this time, but the move is far from random. The RBI has carefully weighed multiple factors before deciding to hold at 5.25%. Here are five reasons behind the decision.

Sensex jumps 3,000 points: What does this mean for stock market investors?The bulls on Dalal Street are back in control for now, but whether they can hold ground will depend on oil, flows and earnings in the days ahead.

Bitcoin above $70,000, altcoins surge up to 23%: Is a new crypto rally starting?The crypto market is showing fresh signs of strength. Bitcoin has reclaimed the $70,000 level, and altcoins are posting strong gains, leaving investors wondering if this momentum can sustain in the coming weeks.

RBI's subtle growth warning is easy to miss, but it's thereRBI's growth warning was subtle, but it was evident in Governor Sanjay Malhotra's monetary policy statement. In this article, we have a closer look at the biggest risks to India's economy.

Sensex, Nifty jump, but IT stocks trade flat. Will they perform post Q4 results?The market rally was broad-based, with strong gains across banking, financial, auto and other sectors. However, IT stocks stood out as the only laggards, trading flat or with marginal gains despite the sharp market rise.

Gold up Rs 5,000, silver jumps Rs 14,000: Will the rally continue?Precious metals are glittering again. In a short span, gold prices have risen by Rs 5,000 and silver has surged Rs 14,000, prompting fresh interest from investors trying to decode whether this momentum will continue.

What is RBI's plan after rupee's recent fall vs US dollar? RBI Governor explainsRBI's focus will remain on managing sharp swings in the currency rather than controlling its direction, as global factors continue to drive movements in the rupee.

RBI keeps repo rate unchanged at 5.25%: How it impacts your EMI and home loan plansIn a welcome move for homebuyers, the RBI has kept the repo rate unchanged at 5.25%. This means no immediate increase in EMIs, offering much-needed relief and stability to borrowers planning their finances.

Elevated energy prices, disruptions at Hormuz likely to impact growth: RBI GuvThe warning comes even as the central bank projects India's economy to grow 6.9% this financial year, signalling that while the growth engine is intact, fresh global shocks are building.

Gold at Rs 1.53 lakh, silver jumps 5%: Why are prices rising today?The rise in precious metals comes alongside a broader rally in equities and a sharp fall in crude oil prices, as investors adjust to changing risk conditions.

entertainment.headline: SRK, Big B, Kamal Haasan, Hrithik: Extreme makeovers Raaka's Allu Arjun reminds of Allu Arjun's half-human, half-animal look in Raaka highlights a growing trend of actors undergoing bold transformations for their roles. From Kamal Haasan's aged makeover to Shah Rukh Khan's rare bald look, such changes reflect the versatility and commitment of actors in Indian cinema.

Viral pic: Prabhas, Sandeep Reddy Vanga's Dhurandhar 2 outing at Allu Cinemas Superstar Prabhas and director Sandeep Reddy Vanga took a break from filming Spirit to watch Dhurandhar 2 in Hyderabad. The spy action film, starring Ranveer Singh, recently broke box office records.

Dhurandhar 2 box office Day 21: Rs 100 crore more to beat Pushpa 2, steady in India Dhurandhar 2 continues its record-breaking run but faces its slowest day yet. With Rs 1,653.67 crore worldwide, can it overtake Pushpa 2 in the coming days?

Why Vignesh Shivan is one of Tamil cinema's most exciting new-age directors around Director Vignesh Shivan is all set to release his new film, Love Insurance Kompany. Here's looking at the filmmaker who has carved a niche with innovation in themes and emotional stories despite setbacks.

How Mammootty was tricked into joining Lokah cinematic universe as Moothon Mammootty discussed his role as Moothon in Lokah and how he was persuaded to join the cinematic universe. The actor voiced Moothon, the leader of the superheroes in the Lokah cinematic universe.

Vikrant Massey opens up on dad guilt, a reason behind his break from films Actor Vikrant Massey took a break from films in December 2024 to focus on his family, especially his son Vardaan. He emphasised the emotional journey of fatherhood and encouraged new dads to embrace their vulnerability.

Samay Raina's Still Alive and question India won't answer: Why can't we take a joke? Samay Raina's Still Alive goes beyond comedy to question India's uneasy relationship with humour after the India's Got Latent controversy. As outrage, censorship and shifting boundaries collide, the bigger question remains: are we really ready to laugh without limits?

Back to the Future's Michael J Fox addresses death rumours Michael J Fox has clarified rumours about his death and is reported to be in good health. The 64-year-old continues to advocate for Parkinson's research while making a notable return to television in the series, Shrinking.

Nikki Glaser says she's ok with boyfriend having sex with others, calls it foreplay Actor-comedian Nikki Glaser opened up about her unconventional relationship with boyfriend Chris Convy. She's fine with physical freedom, but draws a firm line at emotional cheating.

Salman Khan brings back his 90s glasses look, fans say Prem is back Salman Khan has rekindled nostalgia among fans with his throwback airport look and a monochromatic video, reminding many of his iconic past roles. His new style has sparked excitement ahead of his much-anticipated project with director Vamshi Paidipally.

Shah Rukh Khan hypes Atlee's Raaka, can't stop praising Allu Arjun's first look Shah Rukh Khan reacted to Allu Arjun's Raaka first look, calling it 'amazing', as Atlee's ambitious project begins to generate early buzz.

The Boys Season 5 review: Messy and loud, but bloody unmissable finishRelease Date:Wed Apr 08, 2026 The Boys Season 5 review: The final chapter delivers a gripping but uneven farewell, with Antony Starr's Homelander standing out as the show balances satire, shock and emotional stakes.

Nitesh Tiwari shares why Ramayana's Ravana isn't a typical villain Nitesh Tiwari reveals a bold new take on Ravana in Ramayana, challenging his traditional villain image. Here's how the filmmaker plans to reshape the epic narrative.

BTS go rogue with Hooligan music video: A throwback to their 'Dark & Wild' era BTS return with Hooligan, a dark, rap-driven anthem from their reunion album ARIRANG. The dystopian music video marks a bold shift, revisiting their Dark & Wild era with themes of power, rebellion and legacy.

NTRNeel update: No delay in Jr NTR and Prashanth Neel's film, claim makers The makers of NTRNeel clarified the misinformation about the film's schedule cancellation. The film is co-produced by Mythri Movie Makers and NTR Arts.

Pawan Kalyan's wife recalls son's Singapore fire scare, probe still on Actor Pawan Kalyan's wife Anna Lezhneva marks one year of son Mark's fire accident in Singapore, calling it his "second birthday", while also celebrating Akira Nandan's 22nd birthday.

Saurabh Shukla calls Shah Rukh Khan a wise father, shares on-set dynamic Actor Saurabh Shukla opened up about Shah Rukh Khan's approach as a father on the sets of King, revealing how the superstar balances guidance and professionalism with Suhana Khan.

Anurag Kashyap on casting Mrunal Thakur: I have something crazy for her in mind Filmmaker Anurag Kashyap has hinted at a bold, unconventional role for actor Mrunal Thakur in an exclusive interview. He also reflected on her standout performances, saying Thakur often underestimates her own abilities.

general news.headline: US military assets to stay around Iran until real agreement is reached: TrumpTrump said that if the final agreement between Washington and Tehran is not reached, then the US forces might launch larger attacks on Iran.

The nuclear stunner India pulled off; US, France invested billions but failedIndia achieved stunning success at the Kalpakkam nuclear plant, which even the US and France failed to. Only Russia has a commercially running Fast Breeder Reactor (FBR). The Kalpakkam FBR will use India's huge reserves of Thorium to power millions of households, and reduce the country's energy imports.

A govt in denial mode: When distant wars shut local tea shopsIn poll-bound Kerala, the election noise is loud — but the silences are louder. In town after town, familiar small tea shops — the lifeblood of everyday conversation — are shuttered due to the LPG crisis stemming from the Iran war.

Not a credible player: Israel doubts Pak's mediation role in Middle East warIsrael's Ambassador to India, Reuven Azar, questioned Pakistan's credibility despite US outreach, even as Lebanon reeled from its deadliest day in the current Middle East conflict.

Iran sent 3 versions of 10‑point proposal, one probably written by ChatGPT: VanceIran on Wednesday claimed a "historic victory" over the US and Israel after US President Donald Trump agreed to a two-week ceasefire. Tehran also claimed that Washington DC has agreed to its proposals. However, the US has disputed this claim so far.

Iran sets $1 per barrel crypto toll on tankers transiting via Strait of Hormuz: FTUnder the proposed system, tankers would be required to share cargo details via email. Authorities would then calculate a toll -- set at $1 per barrel -- to be paid in digital currencies such as bitcoin. Hosseini noted that vessels would be given only a short window to complete payment.

A sessions court has denied the accused bail, citing the strength of the evidence.

Identity of Satoshi Nakamoto revealed? New report says Bitcoin creator is…Who is Satoshi Nakamoto, the person who created Bitcoin? This has been one of the biggest mysteries in the crypto and tech world. Now, a new report suggests that all clues lead to a British technologist even though the person has denied it again and again.

Live: Voter turnout till 9 am, Assam at 17.87%, Kerala 16.23%, Puducherry 17.41%Assembly Election 2026 Live Updates: Voting is currently underway for the high-stakes Assembly elections in Assam, Kerala and Puducherry, and as well as for the by-polls in Nagaland, Karnataka and Tripura. Polling commenced at 7 am and will end at 6 pm, according to the Election Commission. In Assam, 722 candidates will battle it out for the 126-member Assembly. The contest largely centres on CM Himanta Biswa Sarma's governance record versus the Congress campaign led by Gaurav Gogoi. Kerala sees 2.71 crore voters eligible to vote across 140 constituencies, with 883 candidates contesting. While the race is triangular, it remains primarily a battle between the ruling Left Democratic Front led by CM Pinarayi Vijayan, and the opposition United Democratic Front. In Puducherry, around 9.5 lakh voters will decide the fate of 294 candidates across 30 seats. The NDA alliance is aiming to retain power in the Union Territory, while the INDIA bloc is mounting a strong challenge.

GoPro joins Oracle, Microsoft in layoffs, to cut 23 per cent global workforceGoPro, the action-based camera maker, is set to drastically reduce its workforce. The company stated in a filing that it was going to lay off almost a quarter of its employees as part of an internal restructuring, which is expected to cost it millions.

David Miller hero or villain? Costly call is IPL 2026 Play of the Day in DelhiIPL 2026, DC vs GT: In a contest that swung both ways till the very end, David Miller looked set to take Delhi Capitals home after doing the heavy lifting, only for a momentary lapse to undo all his work, as Gujarat Titans edged out a win by the narrowest of margins to get off the mark on Wednesday.

He also called on Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz for the same period as a goodwill gesture.

sports.headline: PV Sindhu exits in Round of 16 after loss to World No. 2 Wang Zhi Yi PV Sindhu's Badminton Asia Championships campaign ended in the Round of 16 after a straight-games loss to World No. 2 Wang Zhi Yi at the Ningbo Olympic Sports Center on Thursday.

When common sense prevailed in PSL: Iftikhar didn't repeat David Miller's mistake David Miller's refusal to take a single proved costly as Delhi Capitals missed out on a golden chance to beat Gujarat Titans. The moment drew comparisons with Iftikhar Ahmed, whose smarter call in a similar situation helped Peshawar Zalmi win.

Can KKR spark a revival vs LSG or will rain spoil party in Kolkata again? KKR are in desperate need for a revival of fortunes in their IPL 2026 campaign and will hope that the game against LSG can change their season around. With rain threat looming large, will Kolkata be able to find the right combination finally.

Rashid Khan may step away from Test cricket, doubtful for India clash in June Advised by doctors to avoid red-ball cricket after back surgery, Rashid Khan is set to prioritise white-ball formats. The Gujarat Titans spinner all but confirmed his withdrawal from the June Test against India in New Chandigarh.

Miller not at fault: Gavaskar defends DC star's no-single call in 1-run loss Sunil Gavaskar defended David Miller's call not to take a single during the final over of the DC vs GT game. Miller refused to give Kuldeep Yadav strike in the penultimate ball of the final over as DC lost by 1 run.

Vaishali beats Divya to share lead with Jiner Zhu in Candidates Chess India's R Vaishali remained in contention for the Candidates title after beating compatriot Divya Deshmukh in Round 9 on Wednesday, moving into a joint lead with China's Jiner Zhu.

Axar Patel consoles heartbroken Miller as DC lose last-over thriller to GT IPL 2026, DC vs GT: Axar Patel consoled a heartbroken David Miller after the Delhi Capitals fell short by one run against Gujarat Titans in a last-over thriller in IPL 2026 on Wednesday.

IPL 2026: Brave Miller, classy Rahul fall short as GT win thriller to open account IPL 2026, DC vs GT: KL Rahul's sensational 92 and David Miller's heroic cameo went in vain as Gujarat Titans defended 210 by just a run in a last-over thriller in Delhi. Rashid Khan was the hero for the visiting team, which managed to open its account in the new season on Wednesday.

Billie Jean King Cup: Vaishnavi, Sahaja power India to 3-0 sweep over New Zealand Young debutant Vaishnavi Adkar and Sahaja Yamalapalli secured decisive victories as India bounced back to blank New Zealand 3-0 in their second Asia/Oceania Group I tie of the Billie Jean King Cup on Wednesday.

Mohammed Shami devising new variations with Bharat Arun to outfox IPL batters Mohammed Shami outfoxed Abhishek Sharma with a new delivery he had developed just days earlier while working with bowling coach Bharat Arun, Lucknow Super Giants spin bowling coach Carl Crowe revealed.

Cameron Green set to make his bowling return during KKR vs LSG clash in KolkataCameron Green is all set to make his bowling return in the IPL during the KKR vs LSG clash at the Eden Gardens on Thursday, April 9. Green is yet to bowl in the IPL this season in three matches so far as he has failed to justify his price tag so far.

Doha Diamond League postponed until June due to West Asia tensionsThe opening round of the Diamond League season, scheduled to be held in Doha on May 8, has been postponed to June 19 due to the ongoing conflict in the Gulf, organisers said on Wednesday.

Will Narine and Varun return for LSG clash? KKR sweat over key duo's fitnessKKR have issued an update on Sunil Narine and Varun Chakravarthy after the spin duo missed the last clash against Punjab Kings in Kolkata. Narine and Chakravarthy lead KKR's spin attack this season.

Watch: Medvedev loses cool, smashes racket seven times in heavy loss to BerrettiniWorld No. 10 Daniil Medvedev endured one of the most humiliating defeats of his career, going down 0-6, 0-6 to Matteo Berrettini in the third round of the Monte-Carlo Masters. Medvedev, visibly rattled throughout the contest, smashed his racquet multiple times in frustration.

DC vs GT Highlights: Rahul, Miller heroics in vain as Gujarat win thrillerDelhi Capitals (DC) vs Gujarat Titans (GT) Highlights, IPL 2026: KL Rahul's 92 and heroics from David Miller go in vain as GT secure their first win of the season. Rashid Khan's three-wicket haul changed the contest but Miller brought it close with some fireworks in the end.

Ajinkya Rahane defends batting approach amid growing criticism at KKRAjinkya Rahane has pushed back against criticism of his batting approach, asserting that he has always been an instinctive and attacking player despite questions over his strike rate. DC vs GT: Will rain play spoilsport in 3rd straight IPL game?Two back to back matches in the Indian Premier League have been interrupted by rain. Will it be the same for Delhi Capitals vs Gujarat Titans as well?

R Ashwin backs Yashasvi Jaiswal to get Orange Cap in IPL 2026Ravichandran Ashwin has backed Yashasvi Jaiswal to be the leading contender for Orange Cap after his explosive start to the Indian Premier League 2026 season. Jaiswal's consistent, high strike-rate performances have put him ahead early.

CSK CEO updates on MS Dhoni's injury: Will he play team's next match vs DC?Chennai Super Kings will continue to miss MS Dhoni in their next IPL 2026 game. CSK CEO confirmed the news, stating that Dhoni was yet to return to full fitness from his calf injury.

Minerva's historic win vs Liverpool: A Spain trip that almost did not happenMinerva Academy kids created history when they beat Liverpool U15s 6-0 in the MIC Cup, a prestigious international youth tournament. Here's the untold story of how the historic trip almost did not happen.

Ex-India star says coach dependent on DhoniIPL 2026: Stephen Fleming finds himself under pressure as CSK's struggles continue, with questions now being asked about his impact without MS Dhoni leading the side. Bayern's 2014 Neuer comes out of syllabus for Real Madrid in UCL defeatBayern Munich secured a 2–1 win over Real Madrid at the Bernabéu, but it was 40-year-old Manuel Neuer who stole the spotlight with a sensational nine-save performance. Despite Bayern's dominance and missed chances, Madrid remain firmly in the tie ahead of the second leg in Munich.

Beyond cricket's billions: Why hockey's 'El Dorado' lies in speed, not sprawlAs the IPL cements its status as a billion-dollar spectacle, Indian hockey finds itself battling irrelevance, caught between nostalgia and a failure to adapt to modern sport's demands. Can hockey make a comeback in an era of constant visibility, monetisation, and reinvention?

When will J&K pacer Auqib Nabi make his IPL debut?IPL 2026: Delhi Capitals are set to play Gujarat Titans at their own backyard on Wednesday, April 8. Despite acquiring Jammu and Kashmir pacer Auqib Nabi for Rs 8.40 crore, Delhi have not handed him a debut yet in the IPL.

DC vs GT: Gill back for struggling Gujarat as Delhi eye a fortress at KotlaIPL, DC vs GT Preview: Delhi Capitals look to build on their strong start at home as they chase a hat-trick of wins. Shubman Gill's return, meanwhile, offers a timely boost to a struggling Gujarat Titans side.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
